_comment: All tunable knobs for the risk model live here so the logic in risk_model.py stays clean and nothing is hard-coded. Edit weights/thresholds without touching code.

branding._comment: Flip 'mode' to retarget the whole UI with zero code changes. 'generic' is the publishable default; 'coatings' adds the specialty-chemicals flavour. Same engine either way — add your own profile (e.g. a specific company) without touching code.

generic.title: Export Lane Risk Monitor
generic.subtitle: Landed-cost & market risk for a Barcelona-based exporter — 7 global markets, chokepoint stress-tested

coatings.title: Coatings Export Lane Monitor
coatings.subtitle: Landed-cost & market risk for a Spanish specialty-coatings exporter — 7 global markets, chokepoint stress-tested

weights._comment: Top-level component weights, must sum to 1.0. Three of the four are LANE-SPECIFIC (FX, news, chokepoint) so they drive the ranking; fuel cost is a GLOBAL driver (Brent is one world price), so it's intentionally the smallest weight — it shifts every lane together rather than discriminating between them. (A lane-specific container-freight index like Drewry WCI / Freightos FBX would let fuel/freight discriminate too, but those aren't freely available via API.)

fx_volatility._comment: Rolling window (business days) used to measure realized FX volatility. The current 30-day vol is z-scored against the distribution of all rolling-window vols in the fetched history.

fuel_cost._comment: Fuel (bunker) cost risk = Brent crude z-scored vs its trailing average. Oil is the real, live driver of container bunker surcharges; it's a global price, so this sub-score is the same for every lane by design.

scoring._comment: sigmoid_k controls how sharply a z-score is squashed into 0-100. score = 100 / (1 + e^(-k*z)); z=0 -> 50. Higher k = more sensitive. z is clamped to +/- z_clamp first to stop tiny baselines from producing absurd scores. news_sentiment_scale divides the avg keyword net before tanh, so a typical 1-2 net words doesn't instantly saturate the sentiment to +/-1.

chokepoints._comment: Strategic maritime chokepoints with a baseline 'tension' 0-100 reflecting the real mid-2026 climate (Red Sea/Bab-el-Mandeb high from Houthi disruption; Suez elevated). A lane is EXPOSED (risk) to the chokepoints on its natural corridor whether it transits them or is forced to divert. When a corridor chokepoint's tension >= reroute_threshold the lane DIVERTS around the Cape of Good Hope: the map redraws the Cape route, the legend says 'diverting', and a reroute_premium is added to COST. Risk-exposure (0-100 score) and the reroute COST (euros) are two different outputs of the same disruption, not a double-count. Hormuz is oil_linked: it is NOT on any lane's route, but its tension feeds GLOBAL fuel + feedstock cost (coatings are petroleum-derived), so a Hormuz crisis lifts every lane's euro cost.

scenarios._comment: Named stress scenarios. Each overrides chokepoint tensions; the dashboard flips between them and recomputes risk + cost. 'baseline' is the current real climate. 'hormuz_crisis' acts through the GLOBAL fuel/feedstock channel (no lane transits Hormuz), so it lifts every lane's cost rather than any single route's risk.

landed_cost._comment: Estimated all-in EUR cost to land one representative CONTAINER of coatings in each market. container_freight_eur is a lane-specific container freight estimate (a real container index such as Drewry WCI / Freightos FBX is the correct instrument but isn't freely available via API, so these are illustrative, tunable placeholders). base_bunker_eur scales with live Brent vs baseline_oil. tariff_rate is the destination duty (the live US Section 122 10% shows up as real euros; CETA/EU-Mexico land near zero).

news_lexicon._comment: Transparent keyword lexicon for headline sentiment. Each match nudges sentiment toward risk (negative) or calm (positive). Lowercased word-boundary match. Kept in config so the signal is auditable and tunable, not a black box.
